expectations.text: Output has at least 3 markdown H2/H3 sections
expectations.evidence: Many sections: What Are RSC, How They Work, Server vs Client (When to Use), Framework Support, Performance, Gotchas, Mental Model, Quick Decision Guide, Further Reading

expectations.text: Explains the server-side rendering model or how RSC differs from client components
expectations.evidence: Detailed 6-step rendering pipeline, RSC wire format example, hydration model explained

expectations.text: Mentions Next.js or another RSC-supporting framework
expectations.evidence: Full ## Framework Support section covering Next.js App Router, Remix, Expo/React Native, standalone React, Vite frameworks

expectations.text: Provides guidance on when to use vs avoid RSC
expectations.evidence: Detailed 'Use Server Components When' / 'Use Client Components When' sections plus Quick Decision Guide flowchart
## Gotchas and Common Pitfalls with 10 detailed gotchas including serialization, context, 'use client' propagation, async/await React 19 requirement, Server Actions security

notes: Very comprehensive — 344 lines vs 178 for with-skill. Covers more gotchas (10 vs 6), has a performance section, mental model table, and quick decision flowchart. The with-skill version is better focused on the actual question but the baseline is arguably more complete on RSC internals. Both are high quality.
